steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice most effectively uses both sets of notes. Masters et al. shows farm sizes rising across Asia, supporting the farm-size transition hypothesis. Promkhambut et al. shows Thai rice farms stayed small despite modernization. The correct answer must draw on both findings.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Taken together, the studies by Masters et al. and Promkhambut et al. suggest that rice farms in Thailand have responded to the economic pressures associated with modernization by expanding in size.: contradicts Masters et al., which shows rising farm sizes in Asia. ✗
  • Choice B — The predicted shift to large-scale commercial farming may not hold true for rice farms in Thailand, where, according to Promkhambut et al., rice farms have remained small as they've modernized.: relies only on Promkhambut et al. and ignores the broader Asian trend from Masters et al. ✗
  • Choice C — Masters et al. report that the average farm size "already has or will soon begin to rise" in Asia, a finding that is consistent with the farm-size transition hypothesis.: omits the Thai counterexample from Promkhambut et al. ✗
  • Choice D — Although the farm-size transition hypothesis may be applicable to some countries in Asia it is inconsistent with the development of rice farming in Thailand.: synthesizes both notes — affirms the Masters et al. pattern while acknowledging the Promkhambut et al. exception. ✓
That matches Choice D — Although the farm-size transition hypothesis may be applicable to some countries in Asia it is inconsistent with the development of rice farming in Thailand..
